task_completion.description: Format the main title as bold, centered, and at least 16pt font
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Excavation Report: Site 42WN301 (Black Creek Ruin)' at the top of the document is bolded, centered horizontally on the page, and noticeably larger than the standard body text.

task_completion.description: Insert a generated Table of Contents after the author block
task_completion.visual_evidence: A formal Table of Contents block is visible below the title/author area, listing the document's headings along with their corresponding page numbers.

task_completion.description: Apply 'Heading 1' style to the 7 main sections and 'Heading 2' style to the 3 feature subsections
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main sections (e.g., Introduction, Methodology, Stratigraphy & Soils) are formatted with a distinct, large 'Heading 1' style. The feature subsections (Feature 1: Hearth, Feature 2: Midden, Feature 3: Post Hole) are formatted with a 'Heading 2' style, which should be visually distinct from both H1 and standard body text.

task_completion.description: Organize soil layers and levels into a nested list in the Stratigraphy section
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'Stratigraphy & Soils' section, the text describing soil layers and levels is formatted as a bulleted or numbered list with at least two levels of indentation (a nested list structure).

task_completion.description: Convert the pipe-separated artifact data into a 7-column table
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Artifact Catalog' section, there is a properly formatted word processor table with exactly 7 columns (Catalog #, Unit, Level, Artifact Type, Material, Count, Weight (g)), replacing the raw pipe-separated text.

task_completion.description: Convert the raw radiocarbon data into a 5-column table
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Radiocarbon Dating Results' section, there is a properly formatted word processor table with exactly 5 columns (Lab Number, Sample Context, Material, Conventional Radiocarbon Age, Calibrated Date (2-sigma)).

task_completion.description: Set all standard body text to Justified alignment
task_completion.visual_evidence: The standard paragraph text (e.g., in the Introduction or Methodology sections) has flush, even edges on both the left and right margins, indicating justified alignment.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Calligra Words GUI to format the document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Calligra Words application interface being used, rather than a terminal window, python script, or a plain text editor.

integrity.description: Tables and the Table of Contents were generated using the word processor's built-in features
integrity.visual_evidence: The tables have visible cell structures/gridlines typical of word processor tables (not just text aligned with spaces/tabs). The TOC has standard generated formatting like dotted leaders connecting headings to page numbers.

integrity.description: The agent preserved the original text content while formatting
integrity.visual_evidence: Original specific keywords and data (e.g., 'Ancestral Puebloan', '10YR 5/4', 'Beta-489211') are still visible in the document and were not accidentally deleted or replaced with generic placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Main Title: 'Excavation Report: Site 42WN301 (Black Creek Ruin)'. Expected H1 Sections: Introduction, Methodology, Stratigraphy & Soils, Feature Descriptions, Artifact Catalog, Radiocarbon Dating Results, Summary & Conclusions. Expected H2 Sections: Feature 1: Hearth, Feature 2: Midden, Feature 3: Post Hole. Body text samples to check for justification: 'Site 42WN301, commonly known as the Black Creek Ruin', 'Excavations were conducted using standard archaeological procedures', 'The site represents a short-term residential base'. Content keywords that should remain in the document: 42WN-104, Beta-489211, Ancestral Puebloan, 10YR 5/4, Virgin Black-on-white.
